title: Primes in arithmetic progressions to large moduli II: Well-factorable estimates

def:1.summary: Well-factorable sequence $\lambda_q$ of level $Q$: any factorization $Q = Q_1 Q_2$ yields $\gamma^{(1)}, \gamma^{(2)}$ with $|\gamma^{(i)}| \le 1$, $\gamma^{(i)}$ supported on $[1, Q_i]$, $\lambda_q = \sum_{q = q_1 q_2} \gamma^{(1)}_{q_1}\,\gamma^{(2)}_{q_2}$. The classical BFI hypothesis.

thm:A.summary: Bombieri-Friedlander-Iwaniec: well-factorable $\lambda_q$ at level $x^{4/7-\varepsilon}$ gives equidistribution. The level being improved.

def:2.summary: TRIPLY well-factorable sequence: any factorization $Q = Q_1 Q_2 Q_3$ admits the analogous three-fold decomposition. The new structural hypothesis introduced in this paper.

thm:1.1.summary: Triply well-factorable $\lambda_q$ at level $Q \le x^{3/5-\varepsilon}$ gives equidistribution of primes. Improves BFI's $4/7 = 0.5714\ldots$ to $3/5 = 0.6$ — the new BFI-beating level.

thm:1.2.summary: Linear-sieve upper-bound weights $\lambda^+$ at level $x^{7/12-\varepsilon}$ give equidistribution. Direct application of thm:1.1 to the standard sieve weights, since the linear sieve has triply-well-factorable structure.

def:3.summary: Siegel-Walfisz condition for a sequence $\alpha_n$. (Same as PIAP I def:1, restated for self-containment.)

prop:5.1.summary: Well-factorable Type II estimate: triply-well-factorable $\lambda$ at level $x^{3/5-10\varepsilon}$ for the bilinear product $\alpha \star \beta$ with $N \in [x^\varepsilon, x^{2/5}]$. The Type II step in the new range.

prop:5.2.summary: Divisor-function-in-AP estimate: bound for $d_2$ sums in arithmetic progressions, used as input for the Type III decomposition.

prop:8.2.summary: Type III estimate (triple convolution $\alpha_1 \star \alpha_2 \star \alpha_3$) handling the regime where all three factors are essentially $x^{1/3}$. Beyond BFI's range.

prop:9.1.summary: Final assembly: combines prop:5.1, prop:5.2, prop:8.2 to prove thm:1.1. The Type II / Type III balance is what extends past $4/7$ to $3/5$.

edges.note: Linear sieve weights are triply well-factorable.
Lifts BFI's well-factorable level from $4/7$ to $3/5$ (under the strengthened triply-well-factorable hypothesis).
Triply well-factorable strengthens well-factorable by requiring three-fold decomposition.

main_results.description: thm:1.1 ($3/5$ level for triply well-factorable) + thm:1.2 (linear-sieve consequence at $7/12$). The two-line abstract.

structural_definitions.description: def:1 (well-factorable, classical), def:2 (triply well-factorable, new), def:3 (Siegel-Walfisz). The progression of structural hypotheses.

type_estimates_engine.description: prop:5.1 (Type II), prop:5.2 (divisor-AP), prop:8.2 (Type III), prop:9.1 (assembly). The combinatorial machinery.
